text.title: Embeddings Model Selection — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `embeddings-model-selection` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Decision MUST score models on (use case fit, constraints, capabilities). Single-axis picks rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-axis picks (e.g., «highest MTEB score») produce wrong choices for ~40% of teams.

rule.statement: Final pick is validated against your eval set; MTEB leaderboard alone is insufficient.
rule.rationale: MTEB is a proxy; your domain may be tail-heavy and behave differently.

rule.statement: When use case is search/RAG with distinct queries vs documents, prefer asymmetric models (Voyage, Cohere, BGE-M3).
rule.rationale: Symmetric models on asymmetric tasks lose ~10-15% recall.

rule.statement: When cost / storage matters, prefer Matryoshka-capable models that allow dimension truncation.
rule.rationale: Matryoshka models give 60-80% of full-dim quality at 25% of storage cost.

rule.statement: Decision record names ≥2 rejected alternatives with one-sentence reason each.
rule.rationale: Without explicit rejections, the team re-litigates in 3 months.
